current_state.head: d8dc7e845d3a7618fdf41525b67b8bac83d05d28
current_state.hypothesis: One controller(flow).exec call with per-exec tags is one observable activation context; a current-owned session resource resolved by the entry flow is reused by nested executions and isolated from sibling calls.

assignment: DKR-1. Build replayable run-store probes that compare the current ownership model with the proposed activation-context model. Prove or refute: per-exec tags are visible to the entry flow and descendants; the entry flow can resolve one current-owned session resource; nested turn and tool flows reuse it; sibling exec calls do not; close without explicit finish causes no durable business-state transition; checkpoint-close-load-resume works without retaining ExecutionContext. Compare nearest-explicit-context ownership only if a named proof fails. Return the smallest steering decision, not implementation code.
budget_and_stop_rule: At most two probe iterations or 90 minutes. Stop when all named claims are replayable, when one counterexample settles the ownership decision, or when an unknown requires another DKR.
hand_back_rule: Do not redesign cancellation, observation, queue workers, or evidence publication. Hand those unknowns back to their named DKRs.

output_schema.decision_target: current ownership versus a new ownership primitive
output_schema.source_of_truth: saved activation probe and current source
output_schema.decision: candidate only; never accepted by this worker

worker_progress: .okra/runs/issue-triage-session-20260715/workers/dkr-1/progress.jsonl at finish, unknown, or ten-minute heartbeat
in_progress_influence_rule: In-progress worker narrative is not evidence; only worker progress, check-ins, metric reads, flags, or accepted checkpoints can influence the next dispatch.
